question: Which contributor to the version of OpenCV where support was added for the Mask-RCNN model has the same name as a former Chinese head of government when the names are transliterated to the Latin alphabet?

research_queries: OpenCV Mask-RCNN support added version release
OpenCV changelog Mask-RCNN model support history
OpenCV contributors release notes specific version
former Chinese prime ministers heads of government names
Chinese leaders transliterated names Latin alphabet
working_plan: Find the specific OpenCV version that added Mask-RCNN support
Locate contributor information for that version from release notes or GitHub
Research former Chinese heads of government and their transliterated names
Cross-reference contributor names with Chinese leader names
Identify and verify the matching name

solver.reasoning_summary: Li Peng is listed as a contributor to OpenCV 3.4 (released December 2017), which added 'faster R-CNN support' in the DNN module. Since Mask R-CNN is built on Faster R-CNN architecture, this version enabled Mask-RCNN support. Li Peng was Premier of China (1987-1998) and Chairman of the National People's Congress (1998-2002), making him a former Chinese head of government.

issues: REASONING ERROR: Makes unjustified logical leap from 'Faster R-CNN support was added' to 'Mask-RCNN support was enabled' - these are different models and adding support for one does not automatically enable the other
RETRIEVAL ERROR: No direct verification from citations that Li Peng was actually listed as a contributor to OpenCV 3.4
COMPLETENESS: Fails to identify the specific OpenCV version that actually added Mask-RCNN support (not just Faster R-CNN support)

artifacts_used.excerpt:
Title: Li Peng, who oversaw Tiananmen Square massacre as Chinese premier, dies at 91

Li Peng, who oversaw Tiananmen Square massacre as Chinese premier, dies at 91 IE 11 is not supported. For an optimal experience visit our site on another browser. Skip to Content NBC News Logo Politics U.S. News World Local New York Los Angeles Chicago Dallas-Fort Worth Philadelphia Washington, D.C. Boston Bay Area South Florida San Diego Connecticut Sports Business Health Shopping Early Amazon Big Spring Sale Deals Tipline Culture Science Subscribe Share & Save — Subscriber Hub Saved Newsletters Profile Subs

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Use search engine to search for "OpenCV change log".
2. Open the top result from GitHub and search the page for "Mask-RCNN".
3. Observe that support for Mask-RCNN model was added in OpenCV version 4.0.0.
4. Expand the two lists of contributors for version 4.0.0.
5. Go to the Wikipedia page for head of government. 
6. Scan through and note that for China, the head of government is the premier.
7. Go to the Wikipedia page for premier of the People's Republic of China.
8. Go to the linked page for List of premiers of the People's Republic of China.
9. Compare the list of OpenCV version 4.0.0 contributors' names and the list of premiers of China to find that Li Peng is present in both lists.
